documents.title: Introduction | Camoufox
documents.description: Invisible to anti-bot systems 🎭
documents.summary: Camoufox is an open-source, anti-detect browser built on Firefox that focuses on robust, low-level fingerprint injection and rotation to remain invisible to anti-bot systems without resorting to JavaScript tampering.

documents.title: Features List | Camoufox
documents.description: Below is a list of patches and features implemented in Camoufox.
documents.summary: This document lists the features, patches, and optimizations implemented in the Camoufox project, detailing improvements related to fingerprint spoofing, stealth capabilities, anti-font fingerprinting, Playwright support, debloating, addon handling, and a Python interface.

documents.title: Stealth Overview | Camoufox
documents.description: There has been a year gap in maintenance due to a personal situation. Camoufox has gone down in performance due to the base Firefox version and newly
documents.summary: This document explains the internal mechanisms Camoufox uses to evade anti-bot detection by isolating automation code, mimicking realistic user behavior, and rotating consistent browser fingerprints.

documents.title: Python Interface | Camoufox
documents.description: Camoufox's Python library wraps around Playwright's API to help automatically generate & inject unique device characteristics into Camoufox such as the OS,
documents.summary: This document introduces Camoufox, a Python library that provides a lightweight wrapper around the Playwright API to automate the injection of unique device characteristics for browser fingerprint spoofing.

documents.title: Installation | Camoufox
documents.description: First, install the camoufox package:
documents.summary: This document outlines the initial installation process for the `camoufox` package, including an optional geoip dependency, and provides a reference for the available commands within the Camoufox Command Line Interface (CLI).

documents.title: Usage | Camoufox
documents.description: Camoufox is fully compatible with your existing Playwright code. You only have to change your browser initialization:
documents.summary: This document details the available configuration parameters for initializing Camoufox, a library compatible with Playwright for advanced browser fingerprinting control, covering options for device properties, general configuration, and location settings.

documents.title: Passing Config | Camoufox
documents.description: If needed, Camoufox config data can be overridden/passed as a dictionary to the config parameter. This can be used to enable features that have not yet
documents.summary: This document explains how to override or pass custom configuration data, such as WebRTC settings, to the Camoufox client via the 'config' parameter.

documents.title: BrowserForge Integration | Camoufox
documents.description: Camoufox is compatible with BrowserForge fingerprints.
documents.summary: This document explains how Camoufox interacts with BrowserForge fingerprints, covering both the default random generation based on constraints and how to inject custom Fingerprint objects.

documents.title: GeoIP & Proxy Support | Camoufox
documents.description: By passing geoip=True, or passing in a target IP address, Camoufox will automatically use the target IP's longitude, latitude, timezone, country, locale, &
documents.summary: This document explains how to configure Camoufox to automatically use geolocation data from a target IP address for spoofing browser settings like location and language, and provides installation and usage examples using Playwright.

documents.title: Virtual Display | Camoufox
documents.description: While Camoufox includes patches to prevent headless detection, running in headless mode may still be detectable in the future. It's recommended to use a
documents.summary: This document explains how to run the Camoufox library headlessly on Linux systems using a virtual display buffer like Xvfb to improve stealthiness.

documents.title: Main World Execution | Camoufox
documents.description: By default, all JavaScript execution is ran in an isolated scope, invisible to the page.
documents.summary: This document explains how to control whether JavaScript evaluation runs in an isolated scope (isolated world) or the main browser scope to enable DOM manipulation.

documents.title: Remote Server | Camoufox
documents.description: Warning! This feature is experimental. It uses a hacky workaround to gain access to undocumented Playwright methods.
documents.summary: This document explains how to launch Camoufox as a remote websocket server and how clients using the Playwright API can connect to it from other devices or languages.

documents.title: Fingerprint Injection | Camoufox
documents.description: In Camoufox, data is intercepted at the C++ implementation level, making the changes undetectable through JavaScript inspection.
documents.summary: This document explains how to use the Camoufox library, specifically its Python interface, to spoof browser fingerprint properties by passing configuration data.

documents.title: Navigator | Camoufox
documents.description: Navigator properties can be fully spoofed to other Firefox fingerprints, and it is completely safe!
documents.summary: This document outlines the capabilities and safety of spoofing Navigator properties in Firefox, noting specific behaviors and potential leakage points when emulating Chrome.

documents.title: Screen | Camoufox
documents.description: Property
documents.summary: This document lists and describes the properties available on the JavaScript 'screen' object, which provide information about the user's display characteristics.

documents.title: Window | Camoufox
documents.description: Property
documents.summary: This document details a set of properties that allow for the spoofing or modification of various window metrics, including dimensions, scroll positions, and history length.

documents.title: Document | Camoufox
documents.description: Spoof the size of the HTML body element.
documents.summary: This document details the properties available on the document.body object used for determining the client dimensions and borders of the HTML body element.

documents.title: Geolocation & Intl | Camoufox
documents.description: Property
documents.summary: This document describes various properties available for configuration, detailing their types, descriptions, and associated required keys for functionality.

documents.title: HTTP Headers | Camoufox
documents.description: Property
documents.summary: This document lists the specific network headers that Camoufox is capable of overriding within its configuration settings.

documents.title: WebRTC IP | Camoufox
documents.description: Camoufox implements WebRTC IP spoofing at the protocol level by modifying ICE candidates and SDP before they're sent.
documents.summary: This document details the specific properties used by Camoufox to configure WebRTC IP spoofing by modifying ICE candidates and SDP.

documents.title: WebGL | Camoufox
documents.description: As more users visit this site and opt into helping Camoufox's WebGL research, I will have a more solid profile of each GPU's parameters, supported
documents.summary: This document describes the Camoufox WebGL research initiative and provides access to a demo site used for generating WebGL fingerprints from various devices.

documents.title: Media & Audio | Camoufox
documents.description: Spoof the amount of microphones, webcams, and speakers avaliable.
documents.summary: This document details properties used to spoof or fake the available media devices (microphones, webcams, speakers) and parameters of the AudioContext for testing purposes.

documents.title: Voices | Camoufox
documents.description: Spoof the voices used in the browser's Web Speech API.
documents.summary: This document discusses methods for spoofing voices within the Web Speech API, noting a limitation with the `voices:fakeCompletion` feature.

documents.title: Fonts | Camoufox
documents.description: Fonts can be passed to be used in Camoufox through the fonts config property.
documents.summary: This document appears to be a comprehensive list of font names, including various system, UI, and specialized font families available or referenced.

documents.title: Addons | Camoufox
documents.description: In the Camoufox Python library, addons can be loaded with the addons parameter.
documents.summary: This document explains how to load custom browser extensions using the `addons` parameter in the Camoufox Python library, and how to exclude default extensions like uBlock Origin using `exclude_addons`.

documents.title: Cursor Movement | Camoufox
documents.description: Camoufox has built-in support for human-like cursor movement. The natural motion algorithm was originally from riflosnake's HumanCursor, but has been
documents.summary: This document explains the configuration and properties available for enabling and customizing human-like cursor movement within the Camoufox framework.

documents.title: Miscellaneous | Camoufox
documents.description: Property
documents.summary: This document details properties used to spoof or simulate battery status information and the availability of a PDF viewer within a browser environment.

documents.title: Development | Camoufox
documents.description: The following content is intended for those interested in building & debugging Camoufox.
documents.summary: This document provides an overview of the procedures for building Camoufox from scratch and the necessary steps and tools for developing and debugging patches for the software.

documents.title: Build System | Camoufox
documents.description: Here is a diagram of the build system, and its associated make commands:
documents.summary: This document presents a textual diagram illustrating the structure and associated make commands of a software build system, noting its origin from the LibreWolf build system.

documents.title: Building in CLI | Camoufox
documents.description: Camoufox's build system is designed to be used in Linux. WSL will not work!
documents.summary: This document outlines the command-line options available for executing a multi-platform build script, specifying targets and architectures.

documents.title: Building in Docker | Camoufox
documents.description: To build Camoufox on a non-Linux system you can use Docker.
documents.summary: This document outlines the steps required to build Camoufox patches on non-Linux systems using Docker, including how to build the image and execute the build process for specified targets and architectures.

documents.title: Development Tools | Camoufox
documents.description: This repo comes with a developer UI under scripts/developer.py:
documents.summary: This document explains how to use the included developer UI script to create, manage, and modify patches by resetting the workspace and writing changes back to a patch file.

documents.title: Leak Debugging | Camoufox
documents.description: This is a flow chart demonstrating my process for determining leaks without deobfuscating WAF Javascript. The method incrementally reintroduces Camoufox's
documents.summary: This document provides a step-by-step flowchart and associated build commands for incrementally isolating a website flagging issue caused by Camoufox features within a custom Firefox build.

documents.title: About Me | Camoufox
documents.description: I'm a 19 year old security researcher and student at the University of Texas at Austin, currently on medical leave.
documents.summary: This document serves as a personal page for a security researcher, detailing their current status, hobbies like homelab work and tool development, future commitment to projects, and contact information including email and PGP keys.
